channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli strikes on northern Gaza leave at least 87 dead or missing, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes on multiple homes in the northern Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday left at least 87 people dead or missing, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Indonesia swears in ex-general Prabowo Subianto as president
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Prabowo Subianto was inaugurated Sunday as the eighth president of the world’s most populous Muslim-majority nation, completing his journey from an ex-general accused of rights abuses during the dark days of Indonesia’s military dictatorship to the presidential palace.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greek man convicted for sneaking onto neighbors' properties to smell their shoes
item.description: A Greek judge gave a man a suspended one-month prison sentence following his conviction for sneaking onto his neighbors' properties to smell their shoes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house during new barrages with Hezbollah
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's government said a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house Saturday, with no casualties, as fighting with Lebanon-based Hezbollah and Gaza -based Hamas showed no pause after the killing of the Hamas mastermind of last year’s Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Children greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla outside a Sydney church
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted by children Sunday outside a Sydney church in their first public appearance of their Australian visit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — The family of a Guatemalan journalist on Saturday celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for more than two years amid his daily newspaper's anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PEREZ D., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the electrical grid failure that plunged Cuba into darkness
item.description: A few things to know after millions of people in Cuba were left without electricity for two days when the nation's electrical grid collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodriguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Netanyahu promises retaliation following assassination attempt by Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned Hezbollah that its alleged attempt to assassinate him and his wife was a "grave mistake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba hit by second blackout after grid collapse
item.description: Cubans experienced their second black out wave Saturday after government officials said they were working to restore electrical service to hospitals and crucial service centers after a grid failure on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine’s ‘victory plan’ receives mixed reactions from Western allies
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s plan to end Ukraine’s nearly three-year war with Russia has received mixed reactions from Western allies so far.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The first migrants sent to Albania for processing return to Italy
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took back to Italy the first 12 migrants from newly opened asylum processing centers in Albania following a contentious court decision in Rome.
item.dc:creator: VLASOV SULAJ and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major outage leaves all of Cuba without power
item.description: A major power outage has left Cuba without power. The Ministry of Energy and Mines posted on the social platform X at 12:35 p.m. EDT Friday informing the public the National Electricity System was disconnected at 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea's spy agency said North Korea has sent troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, transferring 1,500 North Korean special operation forces to the Russian city of Vladivostok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF video: Israeli tank fires at building where Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was barricaded before being killed
item.description: A building where Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar had been barricaded was targeted by Israeli tank fire before the terror leader was killed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gangs attack neighborhoods across Haiti's capital in new wave of violence
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Multiple gangs attacked several communities in the Haitian capital of Port-au-Prince on Friday, setting fire to homes and a church as bullets whizzed through the area. At least one woman was killed as gangs opened fire in Solino, St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's foreign minister says India's remaining diplomats are on notice not to harm Canadians
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s foreign minister said Friday that India’s remaining diplomats in the country are “clearly on notice” not to endanger Canadian lives after New Delhi’s top envoy in Canada was named a person of interest in the assassination of a Sikh activist.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whelan says Brittney Griner was 'great help' in advocating for his freedom
item.description: Former U.S. Marine Paul Whelan said WNBA star Brittney Griner was of “great help” in advocating for his freedom after being detained in Russia since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Video captures water tank falling on woman — but she miraculously escapes injury
item.description: A woman has escaped injury in Surat, India, after a water tank fell from a roof and landed perfectly around her, a viral video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Uluburun shipwreck was discovered accidentally by a sponge diver
item.description: The Uluburun shipwreck was accidently discovered by a sponge diver in 1982. Following the discovery, excavation of the area took archaeologists a decade to complete.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Roman-era graves found by homeowner during basement renovation project
item.description: During the winter of 2023-2024, archaeologists were called to a home in the suburbs of Paris after a homeowner discovered a skeleton during basement renovations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taliban begins enforcing rule banning 'images of living beings' in Afghan media
item.description: The Taliban is preventing certain outlets from airing images of human beings or animals, according to officials. The rule pertains to certain provinces at the time being.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
